compressed_view: V2 refactor playbook: Phase 0 audit (v2-audit-report.md with P0/P1/P2 severity + tractability tags). Step 1: create v2/ subdirectory next to v1 with identical filename and type name. Step 2: update pbxproj in SAME commit — 4 surgical edits only (new PBXGroup, PBXFileReference, PBXBuildFile, remove v1 from Sources). Step 3: build bottom-up from tokens/components, decompose large v1 files (Training 2135 lines became 533-line container + 6 extracted views). Step 4: mark v1 HISTORICAL in same commit as pbxproj swap. Step 5: walk v2-refactor-checklist Sections A-K. One v2 split per surface, no v3 directories. State.json requires work_subtype=v2_refactor, v2_rule_compliant=true. Anti-patterns: patching v1 in place, Xcode regenerating full pbxproj, starting implementation before Phase 3 UX approval, keeping both v1 and v2 in build target (redeclaration error).

task_signature.context: Step-by-step playbook for performing a UX Foundations-aligned v2 screen rewrite. Derived from Home v2 (PR #61) and Training v2 (PR #74) executions.

learned_patterns.decision: Always produce v2-audit-report.md FIRST. Rate each finding P0/P1/P2. Tag tractability: auto-applicable | needs-decision | needs-new-token | needs-new-component. Home v2 had 27 findings (8 P0, 14 P1, 4 P2). Training v2 had 32 findings (8 P0, 16 P1, 8 P2). Decisions on 'needs-decision' items go into a Decisions Log appended to the audit file.

learned_patterns.pattern: Step 1 — Create v2/ subdirectory at same level as v1
learned_patterns.decision: v2 dir goes NEXT to v1, not in a global folder. Examples: FitTracker/Views/Main/v2/ (Home), FitTracker/Views/Training/v2/ (Training). One v2/ per feature group — never a shared global v2/ folder. File name stays identical: MainScreenView.swift, TrainingPlanView.swift.
learned_patterns.gotcha: Do NOT rename the Swift type. Both v1 and v2 must declare the same struct (e.g. struct MainScreenView: View). The pbxproj swap is what makes one or the other compile — the type name identity means zero call-site changes in parent views.

learned_patterns.pattern: Step 2 — Update project.pbxproj in the SAME commit as the first v2 file
learned_patterns.decision: Four required pbxproj edits: (1) new PBXGroup for v2/ directory, (2) PBXFileReference for v2 file under that group, (3) PBXBuildFile referencing v2 file, (4) v2 PBXBuildFile added to Sources build phase AND v1 PBXBuildFile REMOVED from Sources build phase. v1 PBXFileReference stays (shows in navigator, preserves git history).
learned_patterns.gotcha: git diff main..HEAD -- FitTracker.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj must show ONLY the four edits above. Any UUID reshuffles or unrelated build settings changes indicate a Xcode-regenerated pbxproj — revert and do it surgically. Home v2 completed this in commit ec95eaa (T12).

learned_patterns.pattern: Step 3 — Build v2 file bottom-up from design system, never patch v1
learned_patterns.decision: v1 is read-only during refactor. Start from tokens/components, then assemble the full view. Home v2 bottom-up order: T1 (AppText/AppSize tokens) → T2 (motion tokens) → T3 (Chart tokens) → T4 (AppMetricColumn) → T5 (AppMetricTile) → T6 (HomeRecommendationProvider) → T7 (analytics enums) → T8 (main assembly). Training v2 decomposed to 7 files: container + StatusDropdown + MilestoneModal + ExerciseRow + SetCompletionIndicator + RestTimerView + SkeletonLoadingView.
learned_patterns.gotcha: Large v1 files MUST be decomposed. Training v1 was 2,135 lines; v2 container is 533 lines + 6 extracted views. Home v1 was 1,029 lines; v2 is 703 lines. Do not just move code — refactor the structure.

learned_patterns.pattern: Step 4 — Mark v1 as HISTORICAL in same commit as pbxproj swap
learned_patterns.decision: Add header comment to v1 file: // HISTORICAL — superseded by v2/{ScreenName}.swift on {date} per UX Foundations alignment pass. See .claude/features/{name}/v2-audit-report.md for the gap analysis. This file is no longer in the build target; it stays in the repo as a reviewable reference for the v1 → v2 diff.
learned_patterns.gotcha: Do this in the same commit as T12/T13 (pbxproj + historical marker). Home v2 did this in commit ec95eaa.

learned_patterns.pattern: Step 5 — Walk v2-refactor-checklist.md Sections A-K before Phase 5
learned_patterns.decision: Checklist sections: A (audit/spec), B (file convention), C (token compliance), D (component reuse), E (state coverage), F (accessibility), G (motion), H (analytics), I (project.pbxproj hygiene), J (CI), K (documentation). state.json phases.implementation.checklist_completed must be true before Phase 5 approval. Home v2 completed T14 (checklist walk) after T8+T11+T12.

learned_patterns.pattern: One v2 split per surface — no v3/ directories
learned_patterns.decision: A second alignment pass on the same screen patches v2 in place — NOT a new v3/ dir. The v1→v2 split exists exactly once per surface to capture the first deliberate foundations-aligned rewrite. Home v2 was the second such split (after Onboarding v2 pilot PR #59). Training v2 was the third.
source_executions: CLAUDE.md UI Refactoring & V2 Rule step 6

learned_patterns.pattern: state.json required fields for v2 refactor
learned_patterns.decision: Must set: work_subtype = 'v2_refactor', v1_file_path, v2_file_path (using v2/ convention), v2_rule_compliant = true, checklist_path = 'docs/design-system/v2-refactor-checklist.md'. See home-today-screen/state.json and training-plan-v2/state.json as canonical examples.

anti_patterns.pattern: Patching v1 in place instead of creating v2/ dir
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Onboarding v2 (PR #59) used the older 'patch v1 in place' approach — shipped before this rule existed. Will be retroactively refactored to v2/ convention as a follow-up. Any new refactor that patches v1 directly violates CLAUDE.md and will be rejected in Phase 3 gateway.

anti_patterns.pattern: Regenerating full pbxproj with Xcode instead of surgical edits
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Xcode regeneration shuffles hundreds of UUIDs, making the diff unreviable and potentially breaking other settings. Always edit pbxproj surgically via text editor for exactly the 4 required changes. Verify with git diff.

anti_patterns.pattern: Starting implementation before Phase 3 UX approval
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Phase 3 (ux_or_integration) must be approved before Phase 4 (implementation). The ux-spec.md and compliance gateway (5/5 pass) gate all code writes. Home v2 Phase 3 produced ux-research.md (12/13 principles mapped), ux-spec.md (9 sections), heuristic validation (12/12 pass).

anti_patterns.pattern: Keeping v1 in the build target alongside v2
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Two files declaring the same Swift type in the same build target causes 'redeclaration' compile errors. v1 MUST be removed from Sources build phase when v2 is added. v1 stays as PBXFileReference only.
anti_patterns.source_execution: v2-refactor-checklist B6 — v1 PBXBuildFile removed from Sources

speedup_instructions: When starting a v2 refactor: (1) check this file for the 7-step sequence, (2) use state.json fields pattern to set up the feature state correctly, (3) confirm pbxproj edits match exactly the 4-change pattern before committing. The gotchas in each step are the real time-savers — they encode the mistakes made in Home v2 and Training v2 so you don't repeat them.
